title: Close remaining spec gaps (write-time invariants + legacy cleanup)
summary: Enforce remaining 0.4.0 spec requirements: tx.begin-first/terminal ordering at write-time, intent verified only after tx.verify.pass, and removal or deprecation of legacy replay APIs.
outputs: Write-time enforcement of tx.begin-first and terminal ordering
Write-time and replay enforcement of verified intent sequencing
Legacy replay APIs removed or clearly deprecated
acceptance_criteria: Non-tx.begin events are rejected when no active transaction exists.
Events after tx.end.* are rejected at write-time.
File intent state cannot advance to verified before tx.verify.pass.
Legacy replay APIs are removed or gated so they are not used by resume logic.

steps.description: Enforce tx.begin-first and terminal ordering constraints in write-time validation.
Enforce verified intent sequencing relative to tx.verify.pass in write-time and replay validation.
Remove or deprecate legacy replay APIs and ensure resume paths do not call them.

notes: Prefer explicit validation errors over silent truncation at write-time.
